original_image_tags: Line graph titled "Titan IV" plotting "Breakup Percent (%)" on the y-axis against "Failure Time (sec)" on the x-axis. The y-axis ranges from 0 to 100 in increments of 10. The x-axis ranges from 0 to 280 in increments of 40. Three curves are shown, representing different "q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²" values as per the legend: solid line for "q-alpha = 5,000", dashed line for "q-alpha = 10,000", and dotted line for "q-alpha = 20,000". All three curves show a rapid increase in breakup percentage, peaking near 100% between 40 and 80 seconds, followed by a sharp decline to near 0% by 200 seconds. The q-alpha = 5,000 curve has the broadest peak.

elements.description: Line graph titled 'Titan IV' plotting 'Breakup Percent (%)' (y-axis, 0 to 100) against 'Failure Time (sec)' (x-axis, 0 to 280). It shows three curves corresponding to 'q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²': a solid line for 'q-alpha = 5,000', a dashed line for 'q-alpha = 10,000', and a dotted line for 'q-alpha = 20,000'. The graph is labeled 'Figure 26. Titan IV Breakup Percentages' below the frame.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual elements (line graph, title, axes, three curves with specific q-alpha values and line styles), but Description A includes specific claims about curve behavior and a caption ('Figure 26. Titan IV Breakup Percentages') that Description B omits.
